html:
Exclusive to:
This content is available only after activating the Shadow Rift
Miasma is an environmental hazard that spawns around the Shadow Rift, Dreadstone Outcrops and Fumaroles. The Miasma range increases with the phase of the rift, except for Fumaroles[Verification Needed]. Standing within the Miasma will damage the player by 2 hp every 2 seconds, slow them down, and apply an overlay to obscure their vision.

Applying fire to the Miasma will cause it to dissipate, such as walking through it with a Torch or building a Campfire, Fire Pit, Humble Lamb Idol or casting a dwarf star with Star Caller's Staff. Building a Scaled Furnace, Endothermic Fire or Night Light will not dissipate the Miasma. Additionally, wearing a Desert Goggles, Astroggles or Brightshade Helm will nullify the slowing effect and lessen the intensity of the overlay, but will not prevent the HP depletion. Wearing a Void Cowl will prevent the damage but not the slowing effect.

The Mega Blight consumes Miasma.

Tips

  • It is recommended to avoid the Miasma entirely, as warding it away is only a temporary measure, and one may opt to simply fight the Fused Shadeling that spawn from the Shadow Rift outside of the Miasma instead.
  • Willow’s Flame Cast and Combustion spells dissipate Miasma.

Trivia

  • Miasma was introduced in the Terrors Below update.

Gallery

  • Miasma in the process of dissipating will let off embers as a visual indicator of the process.
  • The overlay applied when one is within the cloud of miasma

{{Object_Infobox
|name = Miasma
|image = Miasma.png
|perk = Can be warded away via Fire
|foodType = [[Food_Type/DST#Miasma|Miasma]] [[File:Miasma.png|32px|link=Food_Type/DST#Miasma]]
|spawnFrom = {{pic24|Shadow_Rift_Phase_1}} {{pic24|Shadow_Rift_Phase_2}} {{pic24|Shadow_Rift_Phase_3}} {{pic24|Dreadstone Outcrop}} {{pic24|Fumarole}}
|damage = 2 hp/2 sec}}
{{Exclusivity|Don't Starve Together}}
{{Rift Content|Shadow Rift}}
'''Miasma''' is an environmental hazard that spawns around the [[Shadow Rift]], [[Dreadstone Outcrop]]s and [[Fumarole]]s. The Miasma range increases with the phase of the rift, except for Fumaroles{{Verify|does it affect fumaroles?}}. Standing within the Miasma will damage the player by 2 hp every 2 seconds, slow them down, and apply an overlay to obscure their vision. 

Applying fire to the Miasma will cause it to dissipate, such as walking through it with a [[Torch]] or building a [[Campfire]], [[Fire Pit]], [[Humble Lamb Idol]] or casting a dwarf star with [[Star Caller's Staff/DST|Star Caller's Staff]]. Building a [[Scaled Furnace]], [[Endothermic Fire/DST|Endothermic Fire]] or [[Night Light/DST|Night Light]] will not dissipate the Miasma. Additionally, wearing a [[Desert Goggles]], [[Astroggles]] or [[Brightshade Helm]] will nullify the slowing effect and lessen the intensity of the overlay, but will not prevent the HP depletion. Wearing a [[Void Cowl]] will prevent the damage but not the slowing effect.

The [[Mega Blight]] consumes Miasma.

== {{pic|32|Prototype}} Tips ==
* It is recommended to avoid the Miasma entirely, as warding it away is only a temporary measure, and one may opt to simply fight the [[Fused Shadeling]] that spawn from the Shadow Rift outside of the Miasma instead.
* Willow’s Flame Cast and Combustion spells dissipate Miasma.

== {{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* Miasma was introduced in the ''[[From Beyond#Terrors Below|Terrors Below]]'' update.
